query: I just finished drafting docs/specs/2026-04-29-billing-export.md for a CSV export of Stripe invoices into our /admin dashboard, can you stress-test it adversarially before I ship?

plan and adversarially review my idea for a single-tenant audit log streamer (push events to s3://logs-prod/audit/ via firehose, retention 7 yrs, SOC2 evidence rows) — write a spec and iterate it to LGTM
iterate this spec to LGTM: docs/specs/2026-04-22-rate-limiter.md — focus on the burst-bucket math, codex flagged it round 1
have Codex stress-test this plan, it's at /Users/me/projects/wfx/docs/specs/2026-04-25-billing-fix.md, want unanimous approve before I touch the code
/planning-loop --revise docs/specs/2026-04-15-rss-mvp.md "focus on rate limiting"

ok i have this messy idea for a slack bot that triages parking_lot.md every monday morning at 9am eastern, posts the digest to #eng-parking, opens a draft PR per cluster — draft me a spec and adversarially review it

draft me a spec from this prose blob and run it through three rounds of codex critique: "webhook receiver for shopify order.create events, validate HMAC, dedupe by event_id, write to postgres orders table, ack 200 within 1.5s, on dlq after 5 retries"
wait — before i implement the migration plan in docs/specs/2026-04-28-pg14-to-pg17.md, i want a third opinion to push back on it. can you run the adversarial review loop on it for me?
the spec at docs/specs/2026-04-26-feature-flags.md — codex said needs-attention three rounds in a row when i tried it manually. can the skill loop fix that for me automatically?
review my spec for typos and grammar — the file is at docs/specs/2026-04-29-blog-cms.md
review the pull request i just opened, i'd like a security pass on the changes in src/auth/
open codex and tell it to review the diff i'm about to push to main, just normal code review
can you write a spec for a billing exporter? just one round, no review loop, i'll handle review myself
what does adversarial review even mean? walk me through codex's review options before i pick one
iterate on this prose paragraph until it sounds less marketing-y — it's the 'about us' page draft
loop through these spec files and tell me which ones are missing an Implementation block: docs/specs/2026-04-*.md
run the planning-loop fixture tests to make sure my changes to lib/auto-apply.sh didn't break anything
retry the codex adversarial-review command on the working tree, the last run timed out
merge docs/specs/2026-04-29-* into a single combined spec and clean up the duplicates before you start implementing
park this side-quest in parking_lot.md: 'investigate why /planning-loop is hitting cap on the rss-mvp spec consistently'
